label: yes

So if you turn around once, you're facing the opposite direction. If you turn around again, you're back to the original direction. A third turn would face you opposite again. So after three turns, you're facing the opposite direction from where you started. So let me visualize this. Let's say I start facing north. I take 7 steps north. Then I turn around once (south), then turn around again (north), then turn around a third time (south). Now I'm facing south. Then I take 1 step south. Then 6 steps. Wait, the instructions are "Take 1 step. Take 6 steps." So total of 7 steps in the opposite direction. So starting point is original position. 7 steps north, then 7 steps south. That would bring me back to the starting point. So the answer would be yes. But let me double-check. Wait, the sequence is 7 steps forward, then three turns (which leaves you facing the opposite direction), then 1 step, then 6 steps. So total steps in the opposite direction: 1 + 6 = 7.
